mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales indicates resilient household spending that supports near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not have higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative growth, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, equity discount rates and refinancing costs rise enough to compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average by more than 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains combined with unemployment below 4.0% signal durable labor income without recessionary slack, supporting consumption and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of t-1mo PAYEMS=156105 and UNRATE=3.7
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger has lower 60-day average returns than non-triggered periods, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward regressions in at least 70% of windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and lower operating leverage, causing earnings expectations to fall and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive months or 3-month change < -0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered industrial-production slowdowns are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts signals improving rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, raising cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises > 5% from the prior 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers are not followed by higher 60-day returns than non-triggered periods, or if the hit rate for positive returns after triggers is below 50% over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with fast CPI growth signal an inflation-scare regime in which expected policy easing is delayed, compressing equity duration and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of t-1mo FEDFUNDS=5.06 and CPIAUCSL=303.294
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate high-inflation trigger has nonnegative average 5-day returns or if adding the FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction fails to improve out-of-sample directional accuracy versus single-series rules.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment from a low base signals early labor-market deterioration and recession-risk repricing, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not lower than unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth signals broad aggregate-demand expansion that supports sales and margins, lowering recession premia and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth is not associated with higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative quarterly growth, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 in two consecutive 3-year windows.
